name: The Millennials Kyoto
detail: This is the only capsule hotel in Kyoto that has a mixed floor, meaning a floor for both guy & girl which is convenient for a couple like us travelling as we don't want to be on different floor for the night. The room is just a bed with all the remote & key being the Ipod that was passed to you during check in. The troublesome thing is to make sure the ipod is fully charged at all time if not, it will affect on how you can control the light, air & stuffs. There is a place for everyone to mingle & eat. Cups & plates are also provided but do remember to do your own washing once you are done.

name: Hotel SUI Kyoto Kiyomizu
detail: There is even a hotel shuttle back to Kyoto station.

name: Livemax Hostels Kyoto Ekimae
detail: Great location excellent price and amazing service

name: GLANSIT KYOTO KAWARAMACHI
detail: I booked the superior capsule and it's the best capsule I had so far in Japan. Unlike most capsule, I don't need to check out of my room if I stay for multiple nights here and there's also option to choose for cleaning service for the superior capsule (no extra charges). It's like a mini hotel room with your own desk and door and bed... but without a lock. To me that's good enough because the price is great! Bed wise is very comfortable, not too noisy with a huge TV screen. It's comparable to a sky suite. Locker is huge enough for you to put your luggage in as well as your bags and others items (you have upper and lower storage space in the locker). The capsule although within about 5-10 mins walk from train station, its location is great as situated opposite Takashimaya and the most happening street in Kyoto and next to Gion. I will definitely come back here again to stay if I want save some costs on my accommodation in Kyoto and yet comfortable enough.

name: FIRSTCABIN Kyoto Nijojo
detail: First Class was spacious comfortable and modern. The hotel was quiet soI had the bathroom and Onsen to myself. Highly recommend, I am going to try some other First Cabin Hotels. It is more professional vs other capsule style hotels or Hostels.

name: APA Hotel Kyoto-Eki-Horikawadori
detail: Hard to find a really cheap and good hotel in Kyoto. Must book hotel in Kyoto as it is so near the Kyoto Train station, good for those using JR pass and those taking bus around Kyoto. Don't forget to buy day pass for bus around Kyoto! (Only 500 JPY)

name: APA Hotel Kyoto Ekihigashi
detail: Clean place, breakfast is ok, no free parking lots and to park overnight for public place is so expensive. Onsen is free and private. There is washing machine, hotel is clean and new condition. There are 7-11 and Lawson close by. The place is close to Kyoto tower and Isetan and Kyoto station. Totally is ok for spending money here.

name: Richmond Hotel Premier Kyoto Ekimae
detail: The location is super convenient, after getting out of central gate of Kyoto station, turn right, going straight for 5 minutes and the hotel is there!! The rooms design is nice, modern and well equipped. The breakfast buffet is also very good. Have no complain and definitely book this hotel again if going to Kyoto.

name: Hotel Elcient Kyoto Hachijoguchi
detail: Specifically booked this hotel due to its proximity to Kyoto Station. Just across the road in fact. Was pleasantly surprised that it is not only near to Kyoto Station, but it is also situated on the JR station side, which means we do not have to walk the length of the station to get to the Shinkansen station to Tokyo. Best located (for my purpose) hotel for me and my small family. There is a kimono rental shop just at the entrance and the selection was small but very beautiful. I personally did not rent from there but from what I can observed, it is a convenient place to start for your kimono experience. They charged around 3900 yen when I was there. There is a small Lawson right across the street from the hotel, in case you came back late from your excursion and in need of refreshment. The shops at Kyoto Station generally close at 10PM. Some earlier. Specifically booked this hotel due to its proximity to Kyoto Station. Just across the road in fact. Was pleasantly surprised that it is not only near to Kyoto Station, but it is also situated on the JR station side, which means we do not have to walk the length of the sta

name: Hotel Resol Kyoto Kawaramachi Sanjo
detail: Our 2nd time in Kyoto. Glad to have found this hotel. Living Lobby, a perfect place for a cup of coffee and creative work. Near to 2 different train stations, three 7-11 outlets with postcard printing machine, 4 different bus stops and countless bars, cafes and restaurants. Walking distance to 祇園 Gion and next to 先斗町 Ponto-chō. Baggage storage meanwhile waiting for the check-in and also check-out. The amazing team of staffs are friendly and very helpful. Our little boy has been feeling under the weather on the 4th day, the staff recommended a clinic nearby. Also during check-out, a taxi has been reserved a day earlier by the reception staff and was informed it would cost around ¥1500 to Kyoto Station. Taxi arrived on-time. Really hassle free, saved us a lot of time too and the cost exactly as been told earlier. For visitors, do consider to grab a taxi from/to Kyoto Station. We would surely return again.
